issue.title: Dep graph parser misses markdown 'Related:' sections — graph mostly empty across the project

successCriteria.description: parseDependencies recognizes inline 'related to #N' and 'see also #N' in addition to the existing depends-on/blocked-by/requires/after vocabulary
parseDependencies finds #N references inside markdown 'Related' sections regardless of list/bullet formatting
Existing tight patterns ('depends on #N', 'blocked by #N', 'requires #N', 'after #N') still parse correctly
Cross-release references are still filtered out (releaseIssueNumbers gating preserved)
Dependency Graph for v0.5.1 shows edges between #147, #148, #149 (since all three reference each other via ## Related sections in their bodies)

phases.title: Extend parseDependencies with inline + section-based recognition
phases.content:
Rewrite `apps/desktop/src/components/dependencies/parseDependencies.ts` to recognize three kinds of references:

1. **Tight inline patterns (existing + extended):**
   ```ts
   const inlinePattern = /(?:depends on|blocked by|requires|after|related to|see also)\s+#(\d+)/gi;
   ```
   Adds `related to` and `see also` to the existing list. All four original triggers stay.

2. **Section-based scan:**
   ```ts
   const sectionPattern = /(?:^|\n)#{1,4}\s*related[^\n]*\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n#{1,6}\s|$)/gi;
   ```
   Matches any markdown heading (h1–h4) whose text contains `related` (case-insensitive). Captures the body following the heading up to the next heading or end of body. Inside the captured block, harvest every `#N` reference via a separate `/#(\d+)/g` scan.

3. **Merge + filter:**
   Collect all parsed numbers into a `Set<number>` (deduplicates). Filter to `releaseIssueNumbers.has(num)` as today. Return as `number[]`.

No edge-type distinction yet (hard vs soft refs both become regular edges). That's a future polish — for now the goal is just to make the graph populated.

Function signature stays unchanged: `(body: string, releaseIssueNumbers: Set<number>) => number[]`. Callers don't need to change.
verification: Read parseDependencies.ts post-edit; confirm both regex patterns are present and the function returns a Set-deduped, release-filtered array
Manual test in node REPL: parse a body containing '## Related\n- #147\n- #149' against a set containing 147, 148, 149 → returns [147, 149]
Manual test: parse 'requires the JSON to already exist' (the false-positive case from #147's body) against any set → does NOT extract any number (since 'requires' is not followed by #N)
Manual test: parse 'depends on #42' against {42} → returns [42] (regression check)
Launch desktop app, open Dependency Graph for v0.5.1, confirm edges between #147, #148, #149 are visible
